admin.no_rulesets: No rulesets have been created yet. Create one to start showing filter rules.
admin.no_providers: No rule providers are registered. Install or enable a provider extension to add rule types.
admin.load_error: Could not load filter rule data.
admin.save_error: Could not save ruleset. Check the form for errors.
admin.delete_ruleset_confirm: Delete the ruleset "{name}"? Its rules will be removed too. This cannot be undone.
admin.reorder_error: Could not save the new order. Showing the latest server state.
admin.toggle_error: Could not update the ruleset's active state.
admin.unsaved_changes_message: You have unsaved changes. Are you sure you want to discard them?

settings.global_evaluate_title_help: When enabled, the rules engine will evaluate the title of the discussion alongside the post content.
settings.global_auto_flag_help: If enabled, triggering this ruleset will automatically create a flag for moderators to review. (Requires flarum/flags)
settings.global_require_approval_help: When enabled, posts will be hidden until approved by the moderation team. (Requires flarum/approval)
settings.global_strict_edit_evaluation_help: Disable Differential Evaluation by default. Edits will be strictly evaluated, preventing grandfathering of approved violations.
settings.global_evasion_active_help: If enabled, the system will track users who repeatedly trigger block rules.
settings.global_evasion_timeout_help: How long the user is held in evasion tracking after triggering a block ruleset. (Default: 5)
settings.global_evasion_threshold_help: Number of blocks required within the timeout period to trigger an evasion penalty. (Default: 2)
settings.global_evasion_log_keep_days_help: Set to 0 to disable log clearing and keep logs forever. (Default: 0)
settings.obfuscate_active_help: If enabled, the JSON payload of the Info and Warning filter rules sent to the browser is XOR obfuscated. This obscures the rules from from casual inspection.
settings.obfuscate_key_help: WARNING: This key is sent to the frontend for decoding. DO NOT use passwords or sensitive secrets!

admin.ruleset_custom_title_help:
Optional. Overrides the default translated title for modals and toasts. Use '{{token}}' to interpolate values returned by rules. Token values are HTML-escaped automatically; the title itself is rendered trusted so you can use simple HTML like '
'. Supports translation keys.
admin.ruleset_scope_tags_help: Select the tags you want this ruleset to apply to. The ruleset will run when the discussion has any of these tags.
admin.ruleset_bypass_groups_help: Users in these groups will bypass this ruleset completely.
admin.ruleset_message_placeholder: Your post contains a restricted word: '{{matched_word}}'
admin.ruleset_evaluate_title_help: When enabled, the rules engine will evaluate the title of the discussion alongside the post content.
admin.ruleset_auto_flag_help: If enabled, triggering this ruleset will automatically create a flag for moderators to review. (Requires flarum/flags)
admin.ruleset_require_approval_help: If enabled, the post will be hidden until approved by a moderator. (Requires flarum/approval)
admin.ruleset_strict_edit_help: If enabled, Differential Evaluation is completely disabled for this ruleset. ANY edit containing a matching violation will be flagged/blocked, even if the post was previously approved with the violation in it. This prevents 'grandfathering'.
admin.ruleset_approval_without_flag_warning: Requiring approval without auto-flagging means the post will be hidden, but moderators will not be notified via the flag queue.
admin.ruleset_exposed_warning_text: Because this intervention does not block the post from saving, the rule configuration is sent to the frontend for evaluation. Although obfuscated, technically-savvy users can decrypt it to see your rules.
admin.ruleset_block_cascade: Stop evaluating subsequent rulesets when this one triggers
admin.ruleset_block_cascade_help: Applies to all intervention types. Use to short-circuit a chain of related rulesets.
admin.ruleset_evasion_active_help: If enabled, subsequent clean posts made shortly after this ruleset blocks the user will be flagged for review.
admin.ruleset_evasion_timeout_help: How long the user is held in evasion after triggering this ruleset block.
admin.ruleset_evasion_threshold_help: Number of blocks required within the timeout period to trigger evasion.
admin.ruleset_flag_message_help: Optional. Define a custom flag or approval reason string for moderators to see when this ruleset triggers. If empty, the default group message will be used. Supports translation keys.
admin.ruleset_evaluate_all_rules_help: If enabled, the engine will evaluate every rule in this ruleset even if a match is already found. This allows you to gather tokens from multiple rules for your custom flag message. If disabled, the ruleset stops evaluating as soon as its condition is met, improving performance.
admin.message_help:
Use '{{token}}' to interpolate values returned by rules. Token values are HTML-escaped automatically; the template itself is rendered trusted so you can use simple HTML like '
'. Supports translation keys.

interventions.info_help: Real-time hint shown while typing. Doesn't block submitting.
interventions.warning_help: Real-time hint shown while typing. Requires user confirmation before the post is submitted.
interventions.block_help: Evaluated server-side at submit. The post is rejected and the user sees the message.
interventions.silent_help: Does not display anything to the user. Evaluated server-side to generate flags/require approval.

displays.none_help: No UI will be shown on the frontend.
displays.banner_help: Full-width strip rendered above the entire composer (inside .App-composer > .container, before #composer). Aligns visually with the editor.
displays.header_banner_help: Narrower strip rendered inside the composer header, directly above the text editor.
displays.sidebar_help: Vertical card pinned to the right of the composer (collapses inline on mobile).
displays.toast_help: Transient notification at the top of the page. Re-appears if the user dismisses it while the rule is still firing.
displays.modal_help: Modal dialog interrupting the user. Shown once per ruleset firing — re-opens only after the rule clears and triggers again.

rule_builder.parse_error_help: Please fix the expression in the editor before switching to the Visual Builder.
rule_builder.editor_help: Write expressions using provider.type operator value. Use AND, OR, and ( ) for grouping.
rule_builder.editor_placeholder: e.g. builtin.contains_word eq ['crypto'] AND (builtin.regex eq '/spam/i' OR ...)

admin.tokens_none: No tokens available — add a rule to see what tokens it exposes.
admin.config_rule_triggered_match_rule_id_help: Select a specific rule to depend on. If selected, other filters below are ignored.
admin.config_rule_triggered_match_name_help: Leave empty to match any rule. Enter text to only trigger if the triggered rule's name contains this text. You can also use regex by wrapping it in slashes, e.g., `/^Rule/i`.
admin.config_rule_triggered_match_scope_help: Only trigger if the matched rule applies to this scope.
admin.config_rule_triggered_match_intervention: Only trigger if the matched rule uses this intervention.
admin.config_rule_triggered_match_display_help: Only trigger if the matched rule uses this display mode.
admin.config_rule_triggered_position_warning: Note: This rule is order-sensitive. It can only detect rules that are positioned ABOVE it in the ruleset list.
admin.config_groups_help: The rule triggers if the user belongs to any of the selected groups. The matched group ID is available as '{{matched_group}}'.
admin.config_words_help: One word or phrase per line. The rule triggers if the post contains any of them (case-insensitive). The first match is available as '{{matched_word}}' in the alert message.
admin.config_patterns_help: One pattern per line. Bare patterns are matched case-insensitively; use PCRE form like /pattern/flags for full control. The first match is available as '{{matched_pattern}}' and '{{matched_string}}'.
admin.config_word_count_help: Note: CJK text (Chinese/Japanese/Korean) is counted character by character. Other text (English, numbers, etc.) is counted by word boundaries (spaces). e.g., 'hello world' is 2 words, while '你好' is 2 characters.
admin.config_exclude_mentions_help: If enabled, mentions like @username will not be counted towards the length limit.
admin.config_exclude_urls_help: If enabled, URLs will be stripped before counting to prevent long links from artificially inflating word counts.
admin.rule_scan_all_help: If enabled, this rule will list every matched word or pattern in the warning message. If disabled, it only lists the first match.
admin.rule_regex_warning: Regex patterns run server-side on every post save. Avoid catastrophic backtracking (e.g. nested quantifiers like (a+)+) — keep patterns simple and well-anchored.

validation.name_required: Please give the ruleset a name.
validation.tags_required: Tag scope is selected — list at least one tag ID.
validation.invalid_ruleset_ids: One or more ruleset IDs are invalid or do not exist.

forum.warning_modal_text: Your post triggered the following warnings. Are you sure you want to submit it?
forum.flag_message: Auto-flagged by Filter Rule Manager. Triggered rulesets: {rulesets}
forum.evasion_flag_message: Suspicious: User was recently blocked by ruleset '{ruleset}', then successfully submitted this post. Please review for filter evasion.
